file: ANCE00199S102_What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S102 What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass
text_en: Different blood vessels may typically be used for the grafts. The internal thoracic arteries in the chest, also known as internal mammary arteries, or the saffonous veins in the legs.

file: ANCE00199S102_What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S102 What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass
text_en: For the internal thoracic artery graft, your surgeon will leave the upper end attached to the subclavian artery, and divert the lower end from your chest wall to your coronary artery, just beyond the blockage. Your surgeon will then sew the graft into place.

file: ANCE00199S102_What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S102 What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass
text_en: For the saffonous vein graft, your surgeon will suture one end to the aorta, and the other end to the narrowed artery, just beyond the blockage.
